root_text.an5.66:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁsājīvo sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an5.66:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attanā ca sīlasampanno hoti, sīlasampadāya kathāya ca kataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.66:1.4: attanā ca samādhisampanno hoti, samādhisampadāya kathāya ca kataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.66:1.5: attanā ca paññāsampanno hoti, paññāsampadāya kathāya ca kataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.66:1.6: attanā ca vimuttisampanno hoti, vimuttisampadāya kathāya ca kataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.66:1.7: attanā ca vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadāya kathāya ca kataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti.
root_text.an5.66:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁsājīvo sabrahmacārīnan”ti.

translation_text.an5.66:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is fit to share their life with their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an5.66:1.3: A mendicant is personally accomplished in ethics, and answers questions posed when discussing accomplishment in ethics.
translation_text.an5.66:1.4: They’re personally accomplished in immersion, and they answer questions posed when discussing accomplishment in immersion.
translation_text.an5.66:1.5: They’re personally accomplished in wisdom, and they answer questions posed when discussing accomplishment in wisdom.
translation_text.an5.66:1.6: They’re personally accomplished in freedom, and they answer questions posed when discussing accomplishment in freedom.
translation_text.an5.66:1.7: They’re personally accomplished in the knowledge and vision of freedom, and they answer questions posed when discussing accomplishment in the knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an5.66:1.8: A mendicant with these five qualities is fit to share their life with their spiritual companions.”
